expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns elsewhere in this repository instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: The response reuses patterns from create-plan, create-tasks, code-architect, and the spec-kit templates rather than presenting an isolated design.

expectations.text: Defines the planning artifact and what information it should contain for handoff.
expectations.evidence: It names a deterministic artifact path .agents/scratchpad/<feature-name>/implementation-brief.md and defines metadata, findings, technical context, assumptions, execution slices, implementation map, validation plan, and a next-agent contract.

expectations.text: Provides a concrete implementation map with file-level changes.
expectations.evidence: It specifies edits to skills/feature-dev/SKILL.md, a new references/implementation-brief-template.md file, updates to code-architect.md, and an optional follow-up in implement-plan/SKILL.md.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: The response explains why the bridge artifact fits the repo better than a full plan/tasks flow for every case and lists trade-offs around overlap and process overhead.

claims.claim: The gap in feature-dev is that the handoff is underspecified rather than absent.
claims.evidence: The response notes that the skill already mentions a reusable handoff plan and implementation brief, but lacks a deterministic path, template, and consumer contract.

uncertainties: Artifact naming and whether downstream implementation skills should consume it directly remain open decisions.
needs_review: Confirm whether Standard and Deep tracks should always write the artifact or only on explicit handoff.
